mnemonics.title: PCA Steps
mnemonics.content: 1) Standardize data 2) Compute covariance matrix 3) Find eigenvalues/eigenvectors 4) Sort by eigenvalues 5) Choose components 6) Transform data - 'SCESS CT' (Standardize, Covariance, Eigen, Sort, Select, Transform)

mnemonics.title: Eigenvalues Meaning
mnemonics.content: Eigenvalues = 'Eigenvalues Encompass Variance' - larger value = more variance captured

mnemonics.title: Why Reduce Dimensions
mnemonics.content: Curse Of Dimensionality - CODD: Computational cost, Overfitting, Distance metrics fail, Data sparsity

acronyms.usage: Transforms correlated variables into uncorrelated principal components
Supervised dimensionality reduction maximizing class separability
Matrix factorization method used for efficient PCA computation
Non-linear technique for high-dimensional data visualization
Preserves distances between points in lower-dimensional space

visualTips.visualization: Draw elbow curve - x-axis = component number, y-axis = eigenvalue. Find 'elbow' where curve bends. Components before elbow are kept.
Think of pie chart - each PC gets a slice. First PC biggest, second next, etc. Keep slices until you have 80-95% of pie.
Imagine rotating coordinate system. Each eigenvector is a new axis. First axis = direction of most spread, second = perpendicular to first with next most spread.

analogies.analogy: Like compressing a 3D object to 2D shadow - keep the shadow that shows the most detail (maximum variance).
Like making notes from a long chapter - condensing to essential points while preserving main ideas.
Like finding the 'essence' of a smoothie - the main ingredients that capture the overall flavor.
